title: Phonics & Word Reading
objective: Students will explore phonics & word reading concepts including Decoding unfamiliar words, Prefixes and suffixes.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Decoding unfamiliar words!
Read Y3-4 exception words with unusual spelling-sound correspondences, applying growing knowledge of morphology and etymology to decode unfamiliar exception words
Time to try this out: Read exception words from the Y3-4 statutory word list accurately (e.g. 'business', 'caught', 'knowledge', 'different')
Can you read trickier exception words — like 'ordinary', 'promise', 'recent', or 'island' — without being thrown off by the parts that don't follow the usual spelling rules?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Decoding unfamiliar words: Read exception words from the Y3-4 statutory word list accurately (e.g. 'business', 'caught', 'knowledge', 'different') Identify unusual letter-sound correspondences in exception words and explain why the word does not follow common GPC rules
Welcome to an exciting lesson about Prefixes and suffixes!
Read words containing common prefixes and suffixes from the Y3-4 programme, applying knowledge of root words, prefixes and suffixes (morphology and etymology) to read aloud and understand new words
Time to try this out: Read words with common prefixes (dis-, mis-, re-, pre-) and suffixes (-ation, -ous, -ly) by identifying the root word first
When you sees a word like 'disappear' or 'comfortable', can they spot the root word and figure out what the prefix or suffix is adding to its meaning?

Amazing work! Let's review what we discovered about Prefixes and suffixes. Read words with common prefixes (dis-, mis-, re-, pre-) and suffixes (-ation, -ous, -ly) by identifying the root word first Apply morphological knowledge to attempt unfamiliar words (e.g. recognise 'happy' inside 'unhappiness')
